name: gatehouse-inner
summary: The fortified room behind a major gate — a portcullis mechanism, murder-holes, guard stations, arrow slits. Built to hold even after the gate falls.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: rectangular, aligned with the gate axis

primary_palette.note: classic gatehouse — portcullis plus its winch, weapons within reach
lit watch-gate — fire for night alarms, weapons for response
interior vault-gate with flanking arrow slits
heavy gatehouse — a mounted ballista aimed down the corridor

story_prompts: manned and alert — guards at post, brazier lit, a dog asleep beside the winch
empty gatehouse, portcullis down, rust on the mechanism
recently overrun — bodies at the winch, portcullis stuck half-raised
ceremonial inner gate, polished and decorative, rarely closed
a dwarf fortress gatehouse with redundant everything — two portcullises, two winches
anti_patterns: don't place the winch away from the portcullis — they're functionally linked
don't miss the defensive orientation — everything faces outward, toward the gate
avoid soft furnishings (carpets, beds, paintings); this is a fighting room
